reason: The BP bound formula r <= floor(sqrt(m)) is the complex Hermitian formula (face dimension = r^2 for rank-r Hermitian PSD matrices). However, the SDP uses real symmetric Choi matrices (justified by time-reversal symmetry). For real symmetric PSD matrices, the face of a rank-r matrix has dimension r(r+1)/2, giving the BP bound r(r+1)/2 <= m, i.e., r <= floor((sqrt(8m+1)-1)/2). The code and paper apply the complex formula to a real-symmetric problem. This is a formula mismatch. Fortunately the complex formula gives a TIGHTER (smaller) bound than the real formula, so if the complex bound holds empirically, the real bound holds a fortiori. But the stated r_BP values are not the correct BP bounds for the actual (real symmetric) optimization.
